title: Add with integer chips
description: Add integers with the support of integer chip diagrams.
topic_path: Topic A: Addition and subtraction of integers and rational numbers

assessment_items.question_content:
The following diagram represents $-3 + (-6)$.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Evaluate.**

$-3+(-6)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
We get a **zero pair** when a $1$ and a $-1$ combine to equal $0$.

This diagram only has negative integer chips, so there are no zero pairs. We can count up the total value.

There are $9$ negative units.

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: Two rows of integer chips. The first row has 3 negative chips. The second row has 6 negative chips.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following diagram represents $-9 + 9$.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Evaluate.**

$-9+9=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
We get a **zero pair** when a $1$ and a $-1$ combine to equal $0$.

The sum will be the value of the leftover chips, after we remove all of the zero pairs.

There are $9$ pairs of positive and negative units.

[[☃ image 1]]

There are $9$ zero pairs.

The zero pairs each equal $0$.

[[☃ image 1]]

There are no positive or negative units left over.

options.alt: Two rows of integer chips. The first row has 9 negative chips. The second row has 9 positive chips.
